mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, forcing equity valuations lower over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 236.0 signals disinflationary pressure that reduces real discount rates, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL drops below 236.0.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01, or 60-day return frequency drops below 50% during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerates indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing that precedes a demand correction, causing negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the industrial production index during a post-recession recovery phase confirms manufacturing resilience, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain negative in over 60% of instances following positive INDPRO revisions in recovery regimes.
